core_mechanism: self-averaging (concentration of measure) of the extremal eigenvalues of a random-matrix ensemble — the sample-to-sample distribution of the largest/smallest eigenvalue collapsing onto a sharply peaked, near-deterministic value as the matrix dimension grows
brief_reason: The abstract's central result is that the ensemble distributions of extreme eigenvalues of large random connectivity matrices converge to peaked distributions as system size increases, which is exactly self-averaging of an extremal spectral statistic in the large-dimension limit.
